Axiomatizing Alex

  1. About Me
  2. Archive

Archive

Posts about learning Lean 4, formal verification, and writing proofs alongside code. I'm documenting what I figure out as I go, mostly focusing on how to apply these ideas to practical programming problems.

Categories

  • Other content
  • Examples of Verso usage

Posts

  • Lean Got Better!
    Alex
    2025-08-14
    • Examples of Verso usage

    Lean has definitely improved in the last couple of years! As I write this, Lean 4.28 is the latest stable release, so let's look at a few of the improvements since 4.20. In Lean, I can define a type of expressions with variables:

    inductive Expr where | var : String → Expr | nat : Nat → Expr | plus : Expr → Expr → Expr

    Given values for the expression's variables, an evaluator assigns a value to the expression as a whole:

    def Expr.eval (ρ : HashMap String Nat) : Expr → Except String Nat | .var x => if let some v := ρ[x]? then pure v else throw s!"{x} not found" | .nat n => pure n | .plus e1 e2 => do return (← e1.eval ρ) + (← e2.eval ρ)

    Even before their variables have values, expressions can be optimized by performing rewrites such as e + 0 ↦ e, 0 + e ↦ e, as well as pre-adding constants:

    def Expr.optimize : Expr → Expr | .plus e1 e2 => match e1.optimize, e2.optimize with | .nat n, .nat k => .nat (n + k) | .nat 0, e2' => e2' | e1', .nat 0 => e1' | e1', e2' => .plus e1' e2' | e => e

    To prove this optimizer correct, we can use induction on its call graph.

    theorem optimize_correct (e : Expr) : e.eval ρ = e.optimize.eval ρ := ρ:HashMap String Nate:Expr⊢ Expr.eval ρ e = Expr.eval ρ e.optimize have : HAdd.hAdd 0 = id := ρ:HashMap String Nate:Expr⊢ Expr.eval ρ e = Expr.eval ρ e.optimize All goals completed! 🐙 ρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide1✝:Expre2✝:Exprn✝:Natk✝:Natx✝¹:e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat k✝x✝:e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat n✝ih2✝:Expr.eval ρ e1✝ = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeih1✝:Expr.eval ρ e2✝ = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimize⊢ Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.plus e2✝) = Expr.eval ρ (Expr.nat (n✝ + k✝))ρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide1✝:Expre2✝:Exprx✝¹:e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat 0x✝:∀ (k : Nat), e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat k → Falseih2✝:Expr.eval ρ e1✝ = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeih1✝:Expr.eval ρ e2✝ = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimize⊢ Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.plus e2✝) = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimizeρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide1✝:Expre2✝:Exprx✝¹:e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat 0x✝:∀ (n : Nat), e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat n → Falseih2✝:Expr.eval ρ e1✝ = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeih1✝:Expr.eval ρ e2✝ = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimize⊢ Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.plus e2✝) = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide1✝:Expre2✝:Exprx✝²:e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat 0 → Falsex✝¹:e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat 0 → Falsex✝:∀ (n k : Nat), e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat n → e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat k → Falseih2✝:Expr.eval ρ e1✝ = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeih1✝:Expr.eval ρ e2✝ = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimize⊢ Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.plus e2✝) = Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.optimize.plus e2✝.optimize)ρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide✝:Exprx✝:∀ (e1 e2 : Expr), e✝ = e1.plus e2 → False⊢ Expr.eval ρ e✝ = Expr.eval ρ e✝ ρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide1✝:Expre2✝:Exprn✝:Natk✝:Natx✝¹:e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat k✝x✝:e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat n✝ih2✝:Expr.eval ρ e1✝ = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeih1✝:Expr.eval ρ e2✝ = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimize⊢ Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.plus e2✝) = Expr.eval ρ (Expr.nat (n✝ + k✝))ρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide1✝:Expre2✝:Exprx✝¹:e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat 0x✝:∀ (k : Nat), e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat k → Falseih2✝:Expr.eval ρ e1✝ = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeih1✝:Expr.eval ρ e2✝ = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimize⊢ Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.plus e2✝) = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimizeρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide1✝:Expre2✝:Exprx✝¹:e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat 0x✝:∀ (n : Nat), e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat n → Falseih2✝:Expr.eval ρ e1✝ = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeih1✝:Expr.eval ρ e2✝ = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimize⊢ Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.plus e2✝) = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide1✝:Expre2✝:Exprx✝²:e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat 0 → Falsex✝¹:e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat 0 → Falsex✝:∀ (n k : Nat), e1✝.optimize = Expr.nat n → e2✝.optimize = Expr.nat k → Falseih2✝:Expr.eval ρ e1✝ = Expr.eval ρ e1✝.optimizeih1✝:Expr.eval ρ e2✝ = Expr.eval ρ e2✝.optimize⊢ Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.plus e2✝) = Expr.eval ρ (e1✝.optimize.plus e2✝.optimize)ρ:HashMap String Natthis:HAdd.hAdd 0 = ide✝:Exprx✝:∀ (e1 e2 : Expr), e✝ = e1.plus e2 → False⊢ Expr.eval ρ e✝ = Expr.eval ρ e✝ All goals completed! 🐙Read more
  • Making Fibonacci Fast (And Proving It's Correct)
    Alex
    2025-07-19
    • Examples of Verso usage

    In my last post, I showed off a simple recursive Fibonacci implementation. While it clearly expresses the mathematical definition, it has a serious performance problem that becomes obvious pretty quickly.

    Read more
  • Writing Technical Posts in Lean
    Alex
    2025-07-12
    • Examples of Verso usage
    • Other content

    I've been thinking about starting a technical blog for a while, and recently decided to try Verso—a documentation tool that lets you write in Lean 4. The appeal is being able to mix explanations with actual executable code and verified proofs rather than relying on screenshots or hand-wavy arguments. Since Lean understands the code directly, you get accurate syntax highlighting, identifiers automatically link to the Lean reference manual, and you can hover over functions to see their documentation and type signatures.

    Here are a couple of examples to show what this looks like in practice.

    Read more
  • Starting This Blog
    Alex
    2025-07-05
    • Examples of Verso usage
    • Other content

    I've been meaning to start writing about technical topics for a while, but kept putting it off. The usual blogging platforms never felt quite right for the kind of content I wanted to create—mixing code examples with mathematical reasoning in a way that actually works.

    Over the past year, I've been learning about formal methods and theorem proving, partly out of curiosity and partly because I think these techniques could be useful for writing more reliable software.

    Read more
